title: callitriche stagnalis subsp. platycarpa
intro: Callitriche stagnalis subsp. platycarpa, also known as water starwort, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It is a submerged aquatic plant that grows in shallow water and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can be used fresh or dried to make herbal preparations, such as tinctures and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health problems.
